title: 3 Legal Considerations and Policies for Incoming and Outgoing Wires
body_text:
3	Legal Considerations and Policies for Incoming and Outgoing Wires
3.1	Legal Considerations for Incoming and Outgoing Wires
Your financial institution must comply with all statutes and regulations pertaining to incoming and outgoing wires, including but not limited to the following:
Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act
(
PCMLTFA
) and
PCMLTF Regulations
Freezing Assets of Corrupt Foreign Officials Act
(Canada)
Criminal Code (Canada)
Human Rights Code (Provincial)
Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act
(
PIPEDA
) /
Personal Information Protection Act
(
PIPA
)
Economic Sanctions
Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for additional information.
3.2	Establishing Wires Policies and Procedures
Your financial institution must establish written policies and procedures for sending outgoing wires, receiving incoming wires, and using the Wires application to ensure regulatory compliance and to mitigate risk.
Ensure all applicable staff receive training appropriate to their roles, both when they begin a role and on a periodic basis thereafter (e.g., at least annually). Policies and procedures must include, but arenât limited to:
Ascertaining the identity of individuals for incoming and outgoing wires
Reporting suspicious transactions
Recording keeping and FINTRAC reporting for incoming and outgoing wires
Reporting terrorist property
Monitoring for split transactions
Making Politically Exposed Persons (PEP) / Head of International Organization (HIO) determinations
Processing foreign currency exchange transactions (i.e., where there is a currency exchange during a transaction such as an incoming wire exchanged into Canadian dollars for deposit, or a US dollar outgoing wire sent from a Canadian dollar account)
Processing transactions for non-account holders
Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information.
Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part
for additional information.
3.3	Establishing Policies and Procedures for Outgoing Wires Monitored in Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM)
Establish policies and procedures for managing outgoing wires monitored in EFM. Such policies can include, but arenât limited to the following:
Communicating with appropriate teams to manage inquiries from customers for wires that are under investigation for potential fraud. If an outgoing wire is alerted as potential fraud in EFM, its status in the Wires application changes to âPendingâ. There is no indication why an outgoing wire has a Pending status. However, authorized EFM users have access to the details of wires that are under investigation.
Communicating with appropriate teams for outgoing wires identified as fraud in EFM. If an outgoing wire is identified as fraud in EFM, its status in the Wires application changes to âCancelledâ. There is no indication why the outgoing wire was cancelled. However, authorized EFM users have access to details of outgoing wires that have been identified as fraud.
Note:	The Wires application isnât connected to your banking system. Follow your internal procedures to settle with the sender.
See Section 14.5, Outgoing Wires Monitored in Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM), and the Enterprise Fraud Management Part for more information.
3.4	Internal Controls and Wires User Management
Your financial institution is responsible for Wires security practices and internal controls. Operations staff, external auditors, and internal auditors must establish internal controls for Wires access. Policies relating to the use of Wires must be established to prevent or detect errors in a timely manner and to restrict access to Wires.
Such policies can include, but arenât limited to:
Ensuring that new Wires Users and those requesting password resets receive their one-time Client Centre passwords in a secure and confidential manner (for individuals who donât already have a Client Centre User ID)
Identifying and deactivating inactive Wires Users and MTS Administrators
Conducting management reviews and internal audits of Wires access and activity
Limiting Wires access to employees who require Wires to complete their job requirements
Establishing reasonable transaction and daily limits for Wires Users (approvals)
Establishing reasonable authorization limits to ensure that two approvals are required on outgoing wires greater than a certain amount (as specified by your financial institution)
Prohibiting the use of multiple Wires user IDs for an employee
END
